title: pseudolmedia laevis
intro: Pseudolmedia laevis, also known as the false umbrella tree, is a species of plant in the family Moraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating a variety of health issues. Pseudolmedia laevis has been used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. The plant can be prepared in various ways, including as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which are often used to treat a range of health ailments.
